summary: At exact HEAD 926227f9, the implementation satisfies OCR-S-1 through OCR-S-8 as a coherent planning/product contract. The public guarded command selects the closed autonomous DAG, advances the managed-worktree HEAD for implementation and repair, preserves typed stop/resume behavior, binds pr_ready to current-HEAD Gate evidence, and leaves PR creation, merge, waiver, and material external side effects explicit. Production Runtime Connectors and Independent Review Orchestration remain their existing owners and are composed from run-session rather than duplicated. No planning_spec/product_requirement finding remains.
inspection_summary: Inspected the current-head Story, parent roadmap, Architecture, canonical Spec and test plan; traced the public CLI into Guarded Run, the new callback-only run-session policy owner, provider coordinator, managed-worktree HEAD rebind, existing independent-review lifecycle, repair replay, and final prepare; compared connector/review diffs against origin/main; checked exact-head verification, conformance and independent adjudication artifacts; inspected the persisted real-provider capability stop; and reran the owner plus public production-shaped E2E suite (20/20 pass). The prior same-HEAD implementation-completion gap is closed by requireHeadAdvance=true and a pre-fix-failing negative test.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the old public description or default selection might still expose a two-action/no-agent flow -> Final conclusion: the English and Japanese help, canonical request resolver, persisted autonomous profile, provider order, and public CLI E2E consistently expose the seven-action guarded closure while retaining explicit legacy and disable fallbacks.

Initial concern: a provider could report completion without producing the implementation commit required by OCR-S-2 -> Final conclusion: current src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js applies requireHeadAdvance=true to prepare_artifacts, implement, and repair; the new same-HEAD implementation fixture returns no_progress and would fail against the prior implementation.

Initial concern: typed stops might be owner-local and fail to survive the user-facing Run lifecycle -> Final conclusion: the public E2E plus Guarded Run tests preserve runtime_unavailable, human decision, verification_failed, no_progress, quota, timeout, ci_pending, and cancelled state across persistence, rendering, resume, containment, and restart.

Initial concern: autonomous closure might cross the intended authority boundary -> Final conclusion: the closed plan terminates at final_prepare, the policy owner rejects external-authority dependency seams, runtime objectives forbid those actions, and pr_ready still requires current-HEAD pr-prepare readiness; PR create, merge, waiver, deploy, and publish remain explicit.

Initial concern: final integration might duplicate the already merged Production Runtime Connectors or Independent Review Orchestration -> Final conclusion: the new owner imports only timers and receives runtime/Gate/review capabilities as callbacks; the connector diff only transmits the objective into the existing provider prompt, while review changes harden the existing lifecycle's current-HEAD polling/containment path rather than creating a second transport or review owner.

Initial concern: staged roadmap closure might prematurely mark delivery complete or require future PR/CI artifacts for pre-PR acceptance -> Final conclusion: predecessor Stories cite merged PR #372/#377/#382, the final Story and roadmap intentionally remain active, and the separate Post-PR Delivery Closure Record reserves CI import, focused closure commit, rereview, evidence rebind, and explicit execute merge for the delivery phase.

acceptance_assessment.basis: Public bilingual help and public CLI tests describe agent-backed guarded closure and explicit stopping authority.
S-001 uses the public CLI and production action owner to create implementation and repair commits, verify, independently review, replay, and final-prepare in a managed worktree.
final_prepare and the Guarded Run postcondition both require prepared HEAD equality and ready_for_pr_create=true.
The closed plan excludes PR create/merge/waiver and negative boundary tests reject PR, waiver, deploy, publish, and generic material-effect seams.
Substantive E2E and focused tests cover success, resume, decision, verification failure, repair, no progress, quota, timeout, CI pending, and cancel.
Production-shaped E2E proves real Git commits and separate read-only review identities; real self-dogfood persists a pre-mutation capability stop with provider and recovery.
The persisted self-dogfood Run reaches the contract-authorized runtime_unavailable typed terminal rather than claiming an unavailable provider succeeded.
Merged predecessor evidence is canonical, connector/review owners are reused, final Story and roadmap remain active, and post-PR closure is separately staged.
